cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/3b8cab1a-5d00-405e-bc22-be0ce479d6e5/tool-results/ && python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_cd1a985e-records_vault_list_documents-1781602785290.txt'))
docs=json.loads(d['result'])['documents']
for x in docs:
    t=(x.get('title') or '')

        print(x.get('id'), '|', t, '|', x.get('kind'), '|', x.get('uploaded_at'), '|', x.get('related_resource_type'), x.get('related_resource_id'))
"

content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0007\", \"folder\": \"SENT\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Acme Cloud change-order package closed and filed\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nClosing the loop here: the change order and Engagement Letter Addendum #1 are both filed cleanly in Records Vault under AICPA_SQMS_7Y, and the formal scope-expansion record is complete.\\n\\nThe Acme CFO countersign came through without negotiation, so the contingency slot for 2026-05-19 was unnecessary.\\n\\nPlease make sure May 2026 MMA onward tracks the two new scope lines separately: multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC and AR-aging bucket cleanup.\\n\\nThanks,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-22T13:30:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"folder\": \"SENT\", \"sender\": \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review\", \"content\": \"Hi Andrea,\\n\\nI'm sending over the draft change-order proposal for Acme Cloud for your review, with Matthew copied in. The package is based on the FP-2026-04 overrun data, the current notes in the ENG-BUDGET-acme_cloud-FY2026-Q1 annotation queue, and the sustained sales-tax pressure hitting account 225000.\\n\\nI also tied the draft back to the parent document titled \\\"Acme Cloud FY2026 Engagement Letter  -  Outsourced Bookkeeping & Tax.\\\" As drafted, if you approve, we would issue this as a discrete change order together with Engagement Letter Addendum #1.\\n\\nThe proposal covers the added effort around multi-state sales tax work for TX, GA, and NC, along with the AR-aging bucket cleanup that has continued beyond the original scope assumptions. Please let me know what you want revised before we send anything client-facing.\\n\\nThanks,\\nGeorge\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-13T15:42:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_045_tax_regulatory_0006\", \"folder\": \"SENT\", \"sender\": \"ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"priya.singh@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Approved: April sales tax packet and filing submission\", \"content\": \"Hi Priya,\\n\\nAll four filings are approved. content.text:
Now I have the full April trail to mirror:
- **Sofia Halabi** (reviewer) → emailed **Ming Chang** (approver) for sign-off
- April by jurisdiction: NY $12,400 / NJ $6,850 / CT $4,200 / PA $3,950 = **$27,400 total**
- **Priya Singh** (npc_019, preparer) drafted the reclass JE (240000→230000) and uploaded the packet
- PA = first return at post-economic-nexus rate (6% on taxable services); Ming confirmed it was "handled cleanly"

Let me pull the May GL data and verify the PA position is settled.

content.text: The April packet is the only sales-tax doc and its content isn't retrievable, so per-state figures come from a prep "cut" not stored in GL state-tags. Let me check whether the May taxable-services-by-state data was handed to me anywhere (my inbox, Airtable, broader Slack on PA/nexus).

content.content: {"result":"{\"emails\": [{\"email_id\": \"email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"william.white@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Hannah,\\n\\nI reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.\\n\\nPlease treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.\\n\\nYou may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.\\n\\nBest,\\nWilliam\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-11T20:45:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_028_monthly_close_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Draft Acme Cloud FP-2026-05 MAP package for review\", \"content\": \"Hi Jones,\\n\\nPackage looks green otherwise.\\n\\nPlease add one explicit sentence in the gross margin walk that the Datadog reclass is a presentation change, not an underlying operating improvement. Andrea will read the May move differently if that distinction is not stated plainly.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_028_monthly_close_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-06T15:42:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_024_monthly_close_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"margaret.sullivan@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Brookfield FP-2026-05 management accounts draft in progress\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nThe package looks complete to me. The Q2-wrap deductibility note is the right call and I would keep it in exactly as drafted, since it pre-empts the kind of later IRS-style question we would otherwise get during workpaper review.\\n\\nThat closes the loop on Andrea's earlier ask from my side. You have a clear green light to finalize the Brookfield FP-2026-05 management accounts package.\\n\\nBest,\\nMargaret\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_024_monthly_close_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-05T15:48:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0009\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition recommendation for exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on BL-75810CD0FEE4\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nApproved to accept timing for FP-2026-05 on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d tied to BL-75810CD0FEE4 and account 119000. Please leave the underlying exception referenced as-is for now rather than changing the disposition trail until we see whether the June rollforward lands.\\n\\nRevisit this at FP-2026-06 BD3. Please ask Anaya to flag in Slack whether the June engagement-hours rollforward is delivered or still missing so the coordination thread stays live and we can decide then whether the item clears or needs escalation.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0008\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-02T19:12:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.\\n\\nThe CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.\\n\\nMonday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T14:19:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_009_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition approval request: exc_aade06f6129e43 for FP-2026-05 cash-payroll feed drop\", \"content\": \"Hi Blue,\\n\\nApproved. Accept-timing for FP-2026-05 is fine based on the 29 dropped rows, Ryan's note, George's precedent, and Hannah's downstream review.\\n\\nIf the June 2 retry does not clear, re-verify at FP-2026-06 BD3 and confirm whether anything changed in the feed behavior before we revisit disposition. Until then, leave exc_aade06f6129e43 in logged state.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T19:51:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nPartner-sight noted on the $6,068.91 amount and Daniel's proposed accounting path.\\n\\nWhat stands out to me is not just the duplicate posting but the vendor-management gap behind the May 22 manual re-key. If a renewal can be manually re-entered without clear confirmation of the active subscription footprint, that points to a broader controls concern around how vendor changes are being validated before they hit the books.\\n\\nNo change from my side on the accounting mechanics Daniel approved. Please keep the exception in investigating until the correction is prepared and the side-workstream is closed through the IT/CIO check.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T12:27:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nApproved on the disposition path.\\n\\nUse FP-2026-05 and book the correction as DR Accounts Payable - External Vendors 210000 and CR Prepaid Software Subscriptions 131000 for $6,068.91. This should reverse the May 22 manual re-key rather than touch the May 15 auto-renewal entry.\\n\\nI will handle the IT/CIO follow-up on seat count and make sure we confirm there is only one active subscription population going forward. Keep the exception in investigating until the entry is actually prepared and we have that vendor-side confirmation in hand.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T12:25:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nI heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.\\n\\nTheir AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.\\n\\nGiven that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T21:30:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"f9991525e1a14fee9a74092f90441b91\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"maya.streamer@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hello Maya,\\n\\nCould you confirm which items from the Friday compliance deadlines summary should stay on the next-week risk scan as audit-sensitive exposures? I am especially trying to separate anything still waiting on approval or control sign-off from items that are fully on track, so we only carry forward the real risks.\\n\\nBest,\\nJulia\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"87558297851e4abab170ac4cabfd46d4\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T19:45:05Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"7b3f8b3bee6b450daabf7dccd30e6034\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"margot.reyes@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hi Margot,\\n\\nI am pulling together the Friday compliance deadlines review and the next-week risk scan notes, and I need your guidance on the workflow side before I send the final version. If you have seen any recurring approval bottlenecks or items that should be escalated, please let me know. I want to make sure we are only carrying true risk items into Monday's scan and that anything manager-level is flagged now.\\n\\nBest,\\nAnaya\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"87558297851e4abab170ac4cabfd46d4\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T19:35:33Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e7c5431acfdc40e5858e221cc147913c\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"priya.singh@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hello Hannah,\\n\\nI support Tom's approach. For the corporate tax side, please make sure anything carried into next week is also checked against approval status before it is marked as ready for Monday's risk scan. That should help us keep the list focused on items with real timing or control exposure.\\n\\nRegards,\\nPriya\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"3dc8469ce2a54e2a94df4e050144fbe4\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T19:28:15Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"3dc8469ce2a54e2a94df4e050144fbe4\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hi Hannah,\\n\\nFollowing up on the Friday compliance review, I will split the items that are only waiting on a reply from the ones that could still miss a filing or review deadline. I am also checking whether any draft entries or support tied to next week should be flagged now so we are clear on what needs attention Monday.\\n\\nBest,\\nTom\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"f9399d9a1f2243f09b36a9cd0431b2b1\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T19:24:22Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"f9399d9a1f2243f09b36a9cd0431b2b1\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hi Tom,\\n\\nThanks for the update. That sounds right, and it helps to keep the review closed for Friday. Please confirm that you are checking not just the dates, but whether each item is actually ready to file without rework. For next week, keep the risk scan tight around anything still pending approval or control sign-off, and flag any exception that could carry into Monday.\\n\\nBest,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"a78de637bfe04256b7f67c0e5ea6c5fd\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T19:14:54Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"a78de637bfe04256b7f67c0e5ea6c5fd\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hi Marina,\\n\\nUnderstood. I will review the tax-side deadline list against the items still awaiting support or approval, and I will flag any filings that could be affected by exc_143730e61ce742, exc_ae607d93ad364a, or the related reconciliations before we close out today. I will send a short update with any carryover items for the Monday risk scan.\\n\\nBest,\\nTom\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"87558297851e4abab170ac4cabfd46d4\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T19:05:08Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"87558297851e4abab170ac4cabfd46d4\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hi Tom and Hannah,\\n\\nQuick status check from my side: I have finished the Friday compliance deadlines review, and the current week filing and approval items remain on schedule. I do not see any immediate filing exposure. cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/3b8cab1a-5d00-405e-bc22-be0ce479d6e5/tool-results/
python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_cd1a985e-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781602904919.txt'))
j=json.loads(d['result'])
jes=j.get('journal_entries') or []
from collections import defaultdict
rev=defaultdict(float)
cnt=0
for e in jes:
    if e.get('status') not in ('posted','approved'): continue
    for l in e.get('lines',[]):
        a=l.get('account_number','')
        if a in ('401000','402000','403000','404000','405000'):
            rev[a]+= (l.get('credit',0)-l.get('debit',0))
    cnt+=1
print('JEs counted (posted/approved of 50 loaded; total period=58):',cnt)
names={'401000':'Audit & Assurance','402000':'Tax Prep & Planning','403000':'Advisory & Consulting','404000':'Outsourced Bookkeeping','405000':'Other Services'}
for a in sorted(rev):
    print(a, names[a], round(rev[a],2))
print('Advisory+Other (likely taxable-services proxy):', round(rev['403000']+rev['405000'],2))
"

content.text: Now let me generate the packet content and upload it to Records Vault alongside April's packet.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/may_packet.txt << 'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS — FIRM OWN-BOOKS SALES & USE TAX
May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet  (STAGED FOR REVIEW / APPROVAL)
============================================================================

Period:        brookfield_FP-2026-05 (May 2026)
Prepared by:   Hannah Grant — Corporate Tax Senior, 2026-06-16
Reviewer:      Sofia Halabi
Approval:      Ming Chang (Partner) — sign-off pending (filing is the partner's call, not the preparer's)
Status:        STAGED. Cycle and treatment set; final per-state dollar amounts pending the
               May taxable-services cut (see Section 4, Open Item). April figures are NOT
               rolled forward as final.

Filed alongside: "Brookfield April 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet"
                 (Records Vault doc_1af8dfb919a9444d, IRS_TAX_7Y).

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. SCOPE / FILING JURISDICTIONS AND DUE DATES
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   New York (NY)      — return due 2026-06-22
   New Jersey (NJ)    — return due 2026-06-22
   Connecticut (CT)   — return due 2026-06-22
   Pennsylvania (PA)  — return due 2026-06-25
   Taxable-services source period: brookfield_FP-2026-05.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. PENNSYLVANIA TREATMENT  (the "get-it-right" item — reviewed, settled, applied)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   - Brookfield crossed the PA $100K economic-nexus threshold in March 2026. April 2026
     was the FIRST filing month under the post-crossing position.
   - PA is filed at the post-economic-nexus rate of 6% on taxable services. In April this
     was reviewed against the updated PA rate schedule; the PA return came to $3,950 at 6%
     and was approved by Ming Chang as "handled cleanly on the first post-crossing return."
   - May PA is handled on the SAME post-economic-nexus 6% basis — consistent with where PA
     has landed since we registered. I reviewed PA's standing and found nothing unsettled:
     the treatment is established (April precedent + Ming's clean sign-off) and is reinforced
     by the standing May kickoff instruction to "keep tracking PA on the post-economic-nexus
     rate used beginning with the April cycle." PA is therefore applied at 6%, not assumed
     blindly and not reverted to a pre-nexus / no-file position.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. APRIL 2026 COMPARATIVE  (approved — reference basis)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   NY  $12,400   |   NJ  $6,850   |   CT  $4,200   |   PA  $3,950   |   TOTAL  $27,400
   April reclass posted: JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-04-0071 (je_5b8169fa03b04149),
   $27,400  DR 230000 Income Tax Payable / CR 240000 Deferred Revenue - Client Retainers,
   prepared by Priya Singh, approved by Ming Chang.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. MAY 2026 LIABILITY — METHODOLOGY AND STATUS
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Methodology (same as April): sales tax is computed on the per-state May taxable-services
   cut. PA at 6% (post-economic-nexus). NY/NJ/CT at the established rates used in the April
   packet.

   OPEN ITEM (flagged, NOT assumed through):
   The per-state May taxable-services cut (NY/NJ/CT/PA) has not yet been pulled. The Oracle
   GL revenue lines for FP-2026-05 are not tagged by jurisdiction, and the SAP AR subledger
   likewise carries no state dimension — both were checked. Final per-state dollar amounts
   therefore cannot be locked from the ledger alone; they must come from the billing-side
   May taxable-services cut. April figures above are shown only as the comparative basis and
   have deliberately NOT been carried forward as the May numbers.

   On approval of the staged cycle and the PA treatment, the per-state amounts will be
   populated from the May cut and the reclass JE (DR 230000 / CR 240000) prepared before the
   FP-2026-05 BD3 lock (2026-06-03 has passed for close; the reclass will be booked in-period
   per the April pattern), mirroring April.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. ROUTING / NEXT STEPS
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   - Ming Chang: approval requested on (a) the staged May cycle and filing calendar, and
     (b) the PA post-economic-nexus 6% treatment for May.
   - Sofia Halabi: review of the staged packet.
   - Final per-state figures to be appended from the May taxable-services cut before filing.
   - Filing deadlines: NY/NJ/CT 2026-06-22; PA 2026-06-25. Nothing is filed until Ming signs.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/may_packet.txt

input.subject: May 2026 Brookfield sales tax cycle — staged for your approval (PA treatment confirmed)
input.content:
Hi Ming,

The May 2026 Brookfield own-books sales tax cycle is now staged and routed to you for sign-off before anything is filed. The monthly kickoff slipped at the start of June, so I picked it up today — we're still ahead of the deadlines, but I didn't want it sitting any longer.

Staged packet (Records Vault): "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet", doc_9f97a265c9a1 — filed alongside April's packet (doc_1af8dfb919a9444d, IRS_TAX_7Y).

Jurisdictions and deadlines:
- NY, NJ, CT — due June 22, 2026
- PA — due June 25, 2026

Pennsylvania (the item I made sure to get right):
PA is handled on the same post-economic-nexus basis as April — 6% on taxable services — consistent with where PA has landed since we crossed the $100K threshold in March and filed the first post-crossing return in April (which you approved as handled cleanly). I reviewed PA's standing specifically and found nothing unsettled; I've applied the established 6% treatment rather than assuming it through or reverting it.

One open item I want to flag rather than paper over:
The final per-state dollar amounts still need the May taxable-services cut. The Oracle GL revenue lines for FP-2026-05 are not tagged by jurisdiction, and the SAP AR subledger carries no state dimension either (I checked both), so the per-state liability can't be locked from the ledger alone — it has to come from the billing-side May cut. I've deliberately NOT rolled April's figures (NY $12,400 / NJ $6,850 / CT $4,200 / PA $3,950 / $27,400) forward as final; they're in the packet only as the comparative basis.

Requesting your approval on (a) the staged cycle and filing calendar, and (b) the PA post-economic-nexus 6% treatment for May. On your go-ahead, the per-state amounts will be populated from the May cut and the reclass JE (DR 230000 / CR 240000) prepared in-period, mirroring April. Nothing gets filed until you sign off.

Thanks,
Hannah

content.text:
Done. The May sales-tax cycle is now moving and staged for approval. Here's what I found and did.

## What was going on
- The **May kickoff reminder did fire** (June 1, recurring monthly) but was never actioned — and the GL confirms it: there is **no May sales-tax JE** in `brookfield_FP-2026-05`, so nobody had started May. The period is still open and the June deadlines haven't passed, so we caught it in time.
- I reconstructed how April was run (April packet `doc_1af8dfb919a9444d` + the Sofia → Ming email chain): **NY $12,400 / NJ $6,850 / CT $4,200 / PA $3,950 = $27,400**, reclassed DR 230000 / CR 240000 and approved by Ming.

## Pennsylvania — the item you flagged
PA is **settled, and I applied it consistently rather than assuming it through**:
- Brookfield crossed PA's $100K economic-nexus threshold in March; April was the first post-crossing return, filed at **6% on taxable services** and signed off by Ming as "handled cleanly."
- The standing May instruction reinforces this ("keep tracking PA on the post-economic-nexus rate used beginning with the April cycle").
- I reviewed PA's standing across Slack/email/compliance records and found **nothing unsettled** — so May PA stays at 6%, not reverted to a pre-nexus/no-file position.

## The one thing I flagged rather than guessed
I could **not** produce final per-state May dollar amounts honestly: the Oracle GL revenue lines for May aren't tagged by jurisdiction, and the SAP AR subledger carries no state dimension either (I checked both). That per-state split comes from the billing-side **May taxable-services cut**, which hasn't been pulled. So I deliberately did **not** roll April's numbers forward as if they were May's — the packet shows April only as the comparative basis, with the cut called out as the open input to lock before filing.

## Deliverables
1. **Records Vault** — staged "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (`doc_9f97a265c9a1`), same kind/classification/retention as April's, filed right alongside it. Trail stays in one place.
2. **Routed to Ming** for approval (email, cc Sofia Halabi and Priya Singh), requesting sign-off on the staged cycle + PA treatment, with the open item flagged and "nothing files until you sign off."
3. **Tax-team note** in #tax-prep-and-filings that May is moving, with states, deadlines, PA treatment, and the open item.
4. **Dated reminder** on your list for **June 18** covering NY/NJ/CT (6/22) and PA (6/25), with the pre-file checklist (Ming's approval → populate per-state from the May cut → prepare the reclass JE).

The recurring monthly kickoff reminder is left intact so future months still trigger.
